title: andryala pinnatifida subsp. latifolia
intro: Andryala pinnatifida subsp. latifolia, also known as Broad-leaved Andryala, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Andryala pinnatifida subsp. latifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
